phases.name: Prescription
phases.description: A healthcare provider orders medication with specific details (name, dosage, frequency, duration).

steps.name: Prescribe Medication
steps.description: Doctor writes or enters a prescription with drug name, dosage form, strength, frequency, and duration.
steps.automation_notes: E-prescribing systems, clinical decision support (CDS), drug interaction alerts

steps.name: Verify Prescription
steps.description: Review prescription for compliance with medical standards, hospital policies, drug interactions, and patient allergies.
steps.automation_notes: Automated drug-drug / drug-allergy checks, barcode verification

steps.name: Approve Prescription
steps.description: Authorize prescription for dispensation after confirming safety, appropriateness, and formulary compliance.
steps.automation_notes: Auto-approval for standard formulary items with no flags

phases.name: Selection, Acquisition, and Reception
phases.description: The pharmacy selects appropriate medications, acquires them from suppliers, and receives and verifies shipments.

steps.name: Select Medication
steps.description: Choose the appropriate medication from available stock or formulary, considering therapeutic equivalence, cost, and availability.
steps.automation_notes: ERP-linked formulary lookup, auto-substitution suggestions

steps.name: Acquire Medication
steps.description: Place purchase orders with suppliers for medications not in stock. Includes vendor selection, PO creation, and order tracking.
steps.automation_notes: Automated reorder points, EDI with suppliers, demand forecasting

steps.name: Receive Shipment
steps.description: Receive delivery from supplier, verify package integrity, check delivery documentation against purchase order.
steps.automation_notes: Barcode/RFID scanning at receiving dock

steps.name: Verify Shipment
steps.description: Quality assurance check: verify quantities, lot numbers, expiry dates, and product condition against order details.
steps.automation_notes: Automated lot/expiry scanning, serialization verification

phases.name: Storage and Storage Management
phases.description: Medications are stored according to specific requirements (temperature, humidity, security for controlled substances) to maintain accessibility and efficacy.

steps.name: Classify and Shelve
steps.description: Classify medications by category (therapeutic class, controlled substance schedule, temperature requirements) and place on appropriate shelves or in refrigerators/safes.
steps.automation_notes: Automated dispensing cabinets (ADCs), carousel systems, pharmacy robots

steps.name: Monitor Storage Conditions
steps.description: Monitor temperature, humidity, and security for storage areas. Log conditions for compliance.
steps.automation_notes: IoT sensors, automated alerting, continuous logging systems

steps.name: Inventory Management
steps.duration_min: daily cycle counts + periodic full inventory
steps.description: Track stock levels, manage expiry dates (FEFO), conduct cycle counts, and trigger reorder points.
steps.automation_notes: ERP/WMS integration, perpetual inventory systems, AI-based demand forecasting

phases.name: Distribution
phases.description: Medications are distributed from central pharmacy to care units (hospital) or directly to patients (community pharmacy).

steps.name: Prepare Distribution
steps.description: Pick medications for ward/unit orders or patient-specific prescriptions. Build medication carts or fill ADC cassettes.
steps.automation_notes: Pharmacy robots, pick-to-light, automated cart fill

steps.name: Transport to Care Unit
steps.description: Transport prepared medications from pharmacy to ward/unit. Methods: manual delivery, pneumatic tube systems, or automated guided vehicles.
steps.automation_notes: AGVs, pneumatic tubes, delivery tracking systems

steps.name: Stock Care Unit
steps.description: Receive medications at ward, verify against order, and load into ward storage or ADC.
steps.automation_notes: ADC with barcode verification, nurse confirmation scan

phases.name: Dispensing and Preparation
phases.description: Pharmacist prepares and dispenses medication as prescribed, ensuring dosage accuracy and appropriate labeling.

steps.name: Compound / Prepare
steps.description: For non-standard preparations: compound medication (e.g. IV admixtures, pediatric formulations, sterile compounds). For standard items: count/pour and label.
steps.automation_notes: IV workflow management, gravimetric verification, compounding robots

steps.name: Final Verification
steps.description: Independent check of dispensed medication against prescription: right drug, right dose, right form, right label, right patient.
steps.automation_notes: Barcode verification, image recognition, automated dispensing check

steps.name: Patient Counseling
steps.description: Explain medication use, dosage, side effects, interactions, and storage instructions to patient or caregiver.
steps.automation_notes: Printed/digital information sheets, automated reminders for new medications

phases.name: Administration
phases.description: Healthcare professionals administer medication to patients with verification of medication, dose, route, time, and patient identity.

steps.name: Prepare for Administration
steps.description: Retrieve medication from ward stock or ADC. Verify the '5 Rights': right patient, right drug, right dose, right route, right time.
steps.automation_notes: Bedside barcode scanning, eMAR integration, smart pumps

steps.name: Administer to Patient
steps.description: Give medication to patient via prescribed route (oral, IV, IM, topical, etc.). Monitor for immediate adverse reactions.
steps.automation_notes: Smart IV pumps with dose-error reduction, automated documentation

steps.name: Document Administration
steps.description: Record administration in patient's MAR: drug, dose, time, route, administering nurse. Note any patient reactions.
steps.automation_notes: Auto-populated eMAR from barcode scan, voice-to-text notes

phases.name: Monitoring and Waste Management
phases.description: Track patient response, handle expired/unused medications, and maintain regulatory compliance.

steps.name: Monitor Patient Response
steps.description: Observe and document patient response to medication. Assess efficacy and side effects. Adjust treatment as needed.
steps.automation_notes: Clinical decision support alerts, remote monitoring, AI-based adverse event detection

steps.name: Handle Returns and Expired Stock
steps.description: Collect unused or expired medications from wards and patients. Segregate for destruction or return to supplier.
steps.automation_notes: FEFO alerts, automated expiry tracking, secure destruction logging

steps.name: Regulatory Reporting
steps.description: Compile and submit required reports: controlled substance logs, adverse event reports, cold chain compliance, inventory audits.
steps.automation_notes: Automated report generation, dashboard-based compliance monitoring
